reviews.review_msg: Outstanding class, outstanding prof. I took his interdisciplinary course last semester and the stuff I learned in it still rings in my ears. Our school is interdisciplinary and the opportunity to work with artists in other disciplines was totally rad! I'd do it again in a heartbeat. I hear that DMac is retiring.  A huge loss to the school.

Professor MacIntyre was a great surprise as a teacher. It was an interdisciplinary class and he knew all about each discipline and the way work was approached by creators in each discipline. By the end of the semester, I realized what an amazing journey he'd taken us on!! All of us (about 30 in the class) had learned a lot about creativity and art!
Superb!!

I've studied composition with three different teachers and I've found Prof. MacIntyre to be the best. He's clear, helpful and patient. His suggestions are excellent and he's encouraged me to be better than I was. I like him!
one of the best I've ever had!
Cool Prof. who knows his stuff. He is kind and generous in tutorials where the action is. Listen, work hard and show up, you'll do well. He likes students to show up!
I'm a serious fan! He gives incredibly good feedback - if you're listening - and he's very focused for every student. I've enjoyed my work with him over the past three years, He makes it worthwhile!
Outstanding prof! Gives great feedback and thoughtful critique. Love it!
Best prof I've had
never hated a prof so much in my life

I enjoyed this class a lot! I've never worked with other disciplines before and I did three projects with artists from other disciplines in this class. It was a gas! David was a great prof! Fun, focused and he knows his stuff.

David is an outstanding teacher who is fully engaged in teaching. Unlike the previous poster, I've never seen him be rude or insulting to anyone - on the contrary, he's warm, friendly and respectful. I recommend him highly!

This guy was so awful I almost quit after my first week. He was rude and insulting. He thinks he knows everything, but he doesn't even have a doctorate. To be honest, I'm not sure I'd recommend studying composition at SFU. I definitely didn't get what I paid for.

Dr. Macintyre is unpredictable; maybe a little moody. He gets upset sometimes when asked questions and appears to be preferential to his favorite students. He is picky rather than celebratory. Just be careful and be sure you get on his good side.
I'm a big fan of dmac. His collaboration course is one of the best courses I've taken at university. 10/10
One of the best profs I've ever had!
One of the best profs I've had!
best prof I've had!

As many have said he might have great credentials, but he is terrible at what he is paid to do; basically, MacIntyre is an awful teacher. Highly critical, his negativity is like a glooming cloud ready to blot out those who might have reservations. Steer clear of this one...
Love this Prof! Took his course because I was interested and I learned an enormous amount. He's insightful, careful and thoughtful. Really appreciated his humour. I'm taking the next level, for sure!

Threatened by people he thinks are smarter and hateful of people he deems unworthy to take his classes.  Will make special assignments and tests for these students, sometimes telling them to drop out once submitted.  Has told students in class that their work and opinions aren't valid.  Uses false information to back up opinions.  The list goes on.

dmac is a great teacher, very insightful and helpful. He can see what's working in a music composition and tell you why. That's been the best thing about his class. He's fair and knowledgeble. He's got a hawk-eye for detail and a great sense of humour.
My favorite prof! The Simon Cowell of Contemporary Arts, he'll say what he thinks and he's rarely wrong. He's very helpful and his guidance is gold. I loved his class and his teaching.

Amazingly useful and helpful and supportive if you get on his good side. I have seen him tear well-meaning students to bits, though. Sometimes it is just that he has the balls to call students on their lack of commitment or integrity, which is laudable, but can be too hard on some students who genuinely try.

very good teacher! focused and knows his material. I liked the way he cared about me and my work. lol when I read the comment below. Sounds like he's a flesh-eater! Not so. Loved his class a lot and loved his teaching.

Tell the world about this teacher! He's got good energy, high interest about what he's teaching, well knowledged in music dance, theatre, film and visual art. Knows what he's doing as an artist. Friendly, knowledgeable. Like factor is high, so I'm sad to see the a good teacher being pilloried in these stupid forums. He's good - let him teach!!!
Nice if he likes you but randomly chooses people he hates and makes their life a living hell.

I was devastated by his horrible comments that I totally lost faith in myself. I nearly quit the arts altogether.  Luckily I had friends to help me through. I was so angry at him after. I was there 10 some odd years ago and he obviously hasn't learned a thing. Awful awful experience. But I'm still in the arts, so nyah nyah nyah.

made my experience at sfu a living hell at times.  i see no method to his teaching.  very unprofessional and petty for no apparent reason.  reduced students to tears, has been know to lash out at people.  this is what i went into debt for?  twisted, bullying & old fashioned with no grasp of current art movements and aesthetics in general. beware.
Really quite a bad person - will go out of his way to make other's lives difficult and unhappy - all with a smile and feigned concern.  Loves to point fingers and assign blame.
petty little bureaucrat
If you are willing to write music his way, he will be very supportive and nurturing. If you have interests that don't match his, he will be an ass.
Unbelievably unprofessional, close-minded, and arrogant.  Obviously has self-esteem and inadequacy issues he takes out on students under the guise of openness.  Not recommended.

a real wolf in sheeps clothing.  close minded, anachronistic, a very poor ability to work with people.  has bad anger management skills, is excessively athoritarian, yet feels like you should "open up" to him.  an absurdity at the sca.  avoid.

Comes across real nice when you first meet him, but later you soon find out he is a very nasty, negative, and apathetic, making him very difficult to work with. Also his artistic taste is rigid and conservative... Avoid him if you can.
Dismissive and close-minded to new ideas despite his rhetoric otherwise. Has his favorite ideas, favorite ways of doing things, and doesn't like to be challenged on them.
No Comments
The best composition teacher, very insightful
